item.title: Now would be a great time to send US ambassadors to the Middle East
item.description: At a time when many question American leadership, there’s an underrated value in simply showing up

item.title: What Jamie Dimon could learn from the collapse of Northern Rock
item.description: UK bank’s collapse offers a lesson for JPMorgan’s CEO and his idea to overhaul the US’s deposit insurance system

item.title: It’s time for private credit’s spring cleaning
item.description: Business development companies face the perils of trying to set valuation marks amid market turmoil

item.title: UK must act more swiftly to develop stablecoins
item.description: Country risks missing next phase of mainstream adoption now under way

item.title: What’s stopping you getting your financial act together?
item.description: Many people draw up a solid strategy but fail to implement it

item.title: Six lessons for investors on pricing disaster
item.description: Buy The Dip mantras ignore the fact that not all US market vulnerabilities have been accounted for

item.title: When the fairies quarrel, the seasons go haywire
item.description: Shakespeare’s ‘A Midsummer Night’s Dream’, the perfect companion to the British spring, has surprising lessons for gardeners

item.title: The biggest threat to China’s global champions is not Trump
item.description: The most competitive companies are being damaged by an underclass of less productive domestic rivals

item.title: For Oura and Whoop, health has a reasonable chance of being wealth
item.description: The two health technology wearable companies have recently raised money at valuations of $11bn and $10bn respectively

item.title: Elon Musk’s grip on the future of US space exploration
item.description: As SpaceX gears up for an IPO, America should avoid becoming over-reliant

item.title: The quantum computing race is wide open
item.description: Investor money has been flowing into a widening range of companies with differing technologies

item.title: The chips chokehold that could end the AI investment boom
item.description: Taiwan’s control of leading-edge silicon chips lies on a geostrategic faultline

item.title: Listen up: AI’s communication blind spot
item.description: How we hear other humans is more crucial than ever

item.title: Britain is in the grip of the fed-up-niks
item.description: What voters are really being drawn to is confident leadership that appears to offer new solutions

item.title: Reopening the minimum wage debate
item.description: Most research from the US challenges the idea that a statutory pay floor doesn’t lower employment

item.title: The creeping feeling that life is getting too long
item.description: Why our lengthening lifespans are not entirely welcome

item.title: The ceasefire bounce
item.description: And the Hormuz bitcoin tollbooth

item.title: Iran war has exposed the weakness of the dollar
item.description: As Opec members have long understood, it is not a good idea to give users of your product an incentive to find alternatives

item.title: Is the BoJ ready to take Japan back to reality?
item.description: This month’s probable rate increase is in keeping with the moment

item.title: The case for trade, remade
item.description: There are good reasons to believe it is likely to follow the rewiring of global finance earlier this century

item.title: Wirecard trial shows the risks of Germany’s slow legal process
item.description: Financial crime cases can be vulnerable to long delays and procedural gridlock

item.title: Oh! What a weird war for investors
item.description: Buying oil might be the right move now

item.title: Nike’s comeback may not come all the way back
item.description: Rehashing old hits and leaving shop shelves empty has enabled upstarts to steal a march

item.title: An unstable truce in the Middle East
item.description: Iran has been severely degraded but for Trump there are lessons to learn

item.title: The UK needs to make choices on tech strategy
item.description: As with Brexit, public anger could swing behind the idea that we need to take back control
